parameters.text:
Write a detailed developer learning note about one of these topics (pick randomly): Python decorators, Flask blueprints, scikit-learn Pipeline with ColumnTransformer, MySQL indexing strategies, or pandas DataFrame optimization.

Structure it exactly like this:
- 2-3 sentences explaining what you learned and why it matters
- A real, working code example (8-15 lines) showing the concept
- 2-3 sentences on what surprised you or what to watch out for
- 1 sentence on what you want to explore next

Write in first person, casual developer tone. Start with 'Today I learned...'. Use ``` to wrap the code block. Plain text only.

options.systemMessage: You are a developer writing genuine daily learning notes with working code examples. Every note must include a real, copy-pasteable code snippet. Never write placeholder code. Always pick a specific, focused concept. Plain text only.

parameters.text:
Write a detailed developer work note about something you built or debugged today. Pick randomly from: building a trading bot signal system, backtesting a moving average strategy, writing a Flask REST API with error handling, training a Random Forest with cross-validation, or designing a MySQL schema with foreign keys.

Structure it exactly like this:
- 2-3 sentences describing the problem or feature you worked on
- A real, working code example (10-20 lines) showing your solution
- 2-3 sentences on what went wrong first and how you fixed it
- 1 sentence on what's left to do

Write in first person, mid-work tone. Start with 'Been working on...'. Use ``` to wrap the code block. Plain text only.

options.systemMessage: You are a developer writing afternoon work-log notes with real code. Sound like someone deep in a project. Code must contain actual logic, loops, conditions, real library usage. Plain text only.

parameters.text:
Write a detailed developer reflection note about a lesson from today. Pick randomly from: open-source PR workflow lessons, ML model deployment with Flask and Docker, optimizing a backtest pipeline, improving a data preprocessing step, or SQL query performance tuning.

Structure it exactly like this:
- 2-3 sentences on what you reflected on and why it matters
- A real, working code example (8-15 lines) showing the improved version
- 2-3 sentences comparing before vs after
- 1 sentence on tomorrow's plan

Write in first person, calm reflective tone. Start with 'Wrapping up today...'. Use ``` to wrap the code block. Plain text only.

options.systemMessage: You are a developer writing evening reflection notes. Every note must show growth. Code examples should demonstrate a clear improvement or best practice. Sound thoughtful and experienced. Plain text only.
